text: Pins/declares the baseline contract being protected before changing anything — schema, dtypes, grain/row-and-group cardinality, null behavior, and known semantic quirks (e.g. refunds attributed to the original order date).
Treats the output as a contract: explicitly flags that changes to schema, cardinality, or semantics are breaking-by-default for the named consumers (finance dashboard, forecasting model).
Insists on verifying against the observable source — read the actual code/SQL, inspect materialized data, check signatures/registries — rather than reasoning from assumptions or the summary.
Proposes real-data validation / a parity check (row count, group cardinality, null rate, aggregate-sum diff within tolerance) before declaring done — not synthetic fixtures alone.

Keeps change intentional and traceable: no silent semantic change; for the investigation, correctly identifies the INNER JOIN + completed-filter as dropping order-date rows and proposes fixing the producer and replaying rather than patching the data forward.
Explicitly invokes the relevant discipline (contract preservation, source-of-truth, parity/replay, idempotency) instead of just performing the task naively.
